questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A parallel plate capacitor has plate area 100 cm² and separation 2 mm. The capacitance is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: azad ke 4 bche ghainb  jmnbb

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two point charges +4μC and -4μC are placed 20 cm apart. What is the electric potential at the midpoint?
questions.explanation: At the midpoint, V = kq/r + k(-q)/r = 0. The potentials due to equal and opposite charges cancel.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a Carnot cycle, the efficiency of the engine is 40%. If the heat rejected is 600 J, the work done by the engine is:
questions.explanation: Q_rejected = 600J, efficiency = 0.4. Q_absorbed = 1000J, W = 400J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A convex lens has focal length 20 cm. An object is placed 30 cm away. The image distance is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The de Broglie wavelength of an electron moving with velocity v is λ. If the velocity is doubled, the wavelength becomes:
questions.explanation: λ = h/mv. If v doubles, λ becomes h/(m·2v) = λ/2

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which of the following is the product of Markovnikov addition of HBr to propene?
questions.explanation: By Markovnikov's rule, H adds to the carbon with more H atoms (C1), and Br adds to C2 giving 2-bromopropane.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The standard EMF of a cell with E°(cathode) = +0.80 V and E°(anode) = -0.44 V is:
questions.explanation: E°cell = E°cathode - E°anode = 0.80 - (-0.44) = 1.24 V

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: For a first-order reaction, the half-life is 60 s. What fraction of the reactant remains after 3 half-lives?
questions.explanation: After 3 half-lives: (1/2)³ = 1/8 of the original amount remains.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Assertion: Ionization energy of Be is greater than B. Reason: Be has a completely filled 2s orbital which is extra stable.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Be (1s²2s²) has a completely filled 2s subshell which is more stable, requiring more energy to remove an electron than B (1s²2s²2p¹).

questions.subject: Biology
questions.text: Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse of the cell'?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration (oxidative phosphorylation), hence called the powerhouse.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.text: In a monohybrid cross between Tt × Tt, what is the probability of getting tall plants with Tt genotype?
questions.explanation: Tt × Tt gives: TT (25%), Tt (50%), tt (25%). So Tt genotype = 50%

questions.subject: Biology
questions.text: Which process is responsible for the movement of water from cell to cell in plants through osmosis?
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the movement of water molecules across a semipermeable membrane from a region of higher water potential to lower water potential.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.text: Which enzyme in the stomach initiates protein digestion?
questions.explanation: Pepsin, secreted as pepsinogen and activated by HCl in the stomach, begins protein digestion.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The value of ∫₀¹ x²dx is:
questions.explanation: ∫x²dx = x³/3. Evaluating from 0 to 1: 1/3 - 0 = 1/3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A sportsman runs around a circular track of radius 𝑟 such that he traverses the path 𝐴𝐵𝐴𝐵. The distance travelled and displacement, respectively, are
questions.explanation: Displacement = 2𝑟 Distance = 2𝜋𝑟 + 𝜋𝑟 = 3𝜋𝑟

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A disc is rolling without slipping on a surface. The radius of the disc is 𝑅. At 𝑡 = 0, the top most point on the disc is 𝐴 as shown in figure. When the disc completes half of its rotation, the displacement of point 𝐴 from its initial position is
questions.explanation: Displacement = √ (2𝑅)^2^ + (𝜋𝑅)^2^ = 𝑅 √ (4 + 𝜋^2)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A cyclist starts from the point 𝑃 of a circular ground of radius 2 km and travels along its circumference to the point S. The displacement of a cyclist is _______.
questions.explanation: ∴ Displacement = R√ 2 = 2√ 2 = √ 8 km

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body starts moving from rest with constant acceleration covers displacement 𝑆1 in first (𝑝 − 1)seconds and 𝑆2 in first 𝑝 seconds. The displacement 𝑆1 + 𝑆2 will be made in time :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle is moving along a circle such that it completes one revolution in 40 seconds. In 2 minutes 20 seconds, the ratio ∣ disdpilsatcaenmceent ∣ is ........
questions.explanation: 𝑇 = 40 𝑠 If 𝑡 = 2 minute 20 second = 2 × 60 + 20 = 140 𝑠 So, it has completed 3 ^1_2 revolution. Distance travelled = 3 × 2𝜋𝑅 + 𝜋𝑅 = 7𝜋𝑅 Displacement = 2𝑅 ∣ D isDpilsatcaenmcee nt| = 72𝜋𝑅𝑅 = 2_2_2 = 111 7× _7

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Consider the motion of the tip of the second hand of a clock. In one minute (R be the length of second hand), its ......

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car moves with speed 60 𝑘𝑚/ℎ for 1 hour in east direction and with same speed for 30 min in south direction. The displacement of car from initial position is ......... 𝑘𝑚
questions.explanation: Displacement of car = √ 60^2^ + 30^2^ = 30√ 5 𝑘𝑚

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The numerical ratio of displacement to the distance covered is always
options: C) Equal to or less than one
D) Equal to or greater than one
questions.explanation: (c)Since displacement is always less than or equal to distance, but never greater than distance. Hence numerical ratio of displacement to the distance covered is always equal to or less than one.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Which of the following is a one dimensional motion
options: B) Earth revolving a round the sun
C) Motion of wheels of a moving trains
D) Train running on a straight track
questions.explanation: Motion of train on straight track is a one dimensional motion whereas everything else is a two dimensional or three dimensional activity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is moving from rest under constant acceleration and let 𝑆1 be the displacement in the first (𝑝 − 1) sec and 𝑆2 be the displacement in the first 𝑝 sec . The displacement in (𝑝^2^ − 𝑝 + 1)^{𝑡}^ℎ sec. will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The displacement of a body is given to be proportional to the cube of time elapsed. The magnitude of the acceleration of the body is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Speed of two identical cars are 𝑢 and 4𝑢at a specific instant. The ratio of the respective distances in which the two cars are stopped from that instant is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An athlete completes one round of a circular track of radius 𝑅 in 40 𝑠𝑒𝑐. What will be his displacement at the end of 2 𝑚𝑖𝑛. 20 𝑠𝑒𝑐
questions.explanation: Total time of motion is 2 𝑚𝑖𝑛 20 𝑠𝑒𝑐 = 140 𝑠𝑒𝑐. As time period of circular motion is 40 𝑠𝑒𝑐 so in 140 𝑠𝑒𝑐. Athlete will complete 3.5 revolution i.e., He will be at diametrically opposite point i.e., Displacement = 2𝑅.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The equation of a circle with center (2, -3) and radius 4 is:
questions.explanation: Standard form: (x-h)² + (y-k)² = r². With h=2, k=-3, r=4: (x-2)² + (y+3)² = 16

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If the roots of 2x² - 5x + k = 0 are equal, then k =
questions.explanation: For equal roots, discriminant = 0. b² - 4ac = 25 - 8k = 0 → k = 25/8

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A string of length 1 m is fixed at both ends. The fundamental frequency is 100 Hz. What is the wave speed?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The maximum number of electrons in a subshell with l = 3 is:
questions.explanation: For l=3 (f subshell), m ranges from -3 to +3 (7 values). Each orbital holds 2 electrons: 2 × 7 = 14 electrons.

questions.subject: Biology
questions.text: Which of the following is a primary producer in an aquatic ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Phytoplankton are photosynthetic organisms that form the base of the aquatic food chain (primary producers).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A current of 5A flows through a circular loop of radius 10 cm. The magnetic field at the center is:

notes.title: Markovnikov's Rule — Quick Notes
notes.content: Markovnikov's rule: In addition of HX to an alkene, H attaches to carbon with greater number of Hs. Example: HBr + propene → 2-bromopropane.

notes.title: Electrostatics — Key Formulae
notes.content: Potential at midpoint between +q and -q is zero. Capacitance of parallel plate C = ε0 A / d.
